before: Chong channel and Ren channel both originate from the uterus. before: The factors contributing to this disturbance are numerous, but it results mainly from dysfunction of the lungs, spleen, and liver, and obstruction of Tri-Jiao as passageway. Of these factors, the most important one is dysfunction of the spleen in transporation and transformation as the saying goes, "Such syndromes as dampness, swelling and distentim are all attributable to dysfunction of the spleen." By disturbance in discharge of body fluid is meant edema resulting from water retention due to weakened function of in ternal organs to transform fluid into sweat and urine.
after: The factors contributing to this disturbance are numerous, but it results mainly from dysfunction of the lungs, spleen, and liver, and obstruction of Tri-Jiao as passageway. Of these factors, the most important one is dysfunction of the spleen in transportation and transformation as the saying goes, "Such syndromes as dampness, swelling and distention are all attributable to dysfunction of the spleen." By disturbance in discharge of body fluid is meant edema resulting from water retention due to weakened function of internal organs to transform fluid into sweat and urine.

before: For clinical monifestations refer to the section on "the syndrome of deficiency of the liver-blood".
after: For clinical manifestations refer to the section on "the syndrome of deficiency of the liver-blood".

before: For instance, in case of deficiency of blood or deficiency of both Qi and blood resulting from insufficient blood formation due to deficiency of Qi (the function of which is to produce blood), treatment should be aimed mainly at invigorating Qi supplemented by replenishing blood; in case of blood stasis resulting from deficiency or stagnation of Qi (the function of which is to promote blood circulation), also known as blood stasis due to deficiency of Qi or blood stasis due lo stagnation of Qi, treatment be aimed at invigorating Qi to promote blood circulation or at promoting the flow of Qi to activate blood circulation, thereby dissipating blood stasis; in case of syncope or hemoptysis and hematemesis due to bleeding resulting from adverse rising of Qi as a result of its functional disturbance, it is necessary to regulate blood flow by sending down adversely ascending Qi; in case of hemorrhage due to deficiency of Qi which fails to control blood flow, it is advisable to control blood flow by invigorating Qi; and in case of exhaustion of Qi resulting from massive hemorrhage (because deficiency of blood, the mother of Qi, leads to insuficiency of Qi), it is essential to arrest bleeding by invigorating Qi in acute cases on the basis of the principle of treating blood collapse by first supplementing Qi.
after: For instance, in case of deficiency of blood or deficiency of both Qi and blood resulting from insufficient blood formation due to deficiency of Qi (the function of which is to produce blood), treatment should be aimed mainly at invigorating Qi supplemented by replenishing blood; in case of blood stasis resulting from deficiency or stagnation of Qi (the function of which is to promote blood circulation), also known as blood stasis due to deficiency of Qi or blood stasis due to stagnation of Qi, treatment be aimed at invigorating Qi to promote blood circulation or at promoting the flow of Qi to activate blood circulation, thereby dissipating blood stasis; in case of syncope or hemoptysis and hematemesis due to bleeding resulting from adverse rising of Qi as a result of its functional disturbance, it is necessary to regulate blood flow by sending down adversely ascending Qi; in case of hemorrhage due to deficiency of Qi which fails to control blood flow, it is advisable to control blood flow by invigorating Qi; and in case of exhaustion of Qi resulting from massive hemorrhage (because deficiency of blood, the mother of Qi, leads to insufficiency of Qi), it is essential to arrest bleeding by invigorating Qi in acute cases on the basis of the principle of treating blood collapse by first supplementing Qi.
